templates.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client by HTTP
templates.description:
Get HashiCorp Nomad client metrics by HTTP from metrics endpoint.

More information about metrics is available in the official documentation: https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/docs/operations/metrics-reference.

You can discuss this template or leave feedback on our forum: https://www.zabbix.com/forum/zabbix-suggestions-and-feedback.

Generated by official Zabbix template tool "Templator"
templates.readme:
## Overview

This template is designed to monitor HashiCorp Nomad clients by Zabbix.
It works without any external scripts.

## Setup

1. Enable telemetry in HashiCorp Nomad agent configuration file. Set the Prometheus metrics format.
>Refer to the [`vendor documentation`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/docs/configuration/telemetry).
2. Prepare an ACL token with `node:read`, `namespace:read-job` permissions applied. Define the `{$NOMAD.TOKEN}` host wizard configuration field value.
> Refer to the vendor documentation about [`Nomad native ACL`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/tutorials/access-control/access-control-policies) or [`Nomad Vault-generated tokens`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/tutorials/access-control/vault-nomad-secrets) if you're using integration with HashiCorp Vault.
3. Set the values for the `Nomad API scheme` and `Nomad API port` host wizard configuration fields to define the common Nomad API web schema and connection port.

**Additional information**:

* You have to prepare an additional ACL token only if you wish to monitor Nomad clients as separate entities. If you're using clients discovery - token will be inherited from the master host linked to the HashiCorp Nomad by HTTP template.

* If you're not using ACL - skip 2nd setup step.

* The Nomad clients use the default web schema - `HTTP` and default API port - `4646`. If you're using clients discovery and you need to re-define macros for the particular host created from prototype, use the context macros like {{$NOMAD.CLIENT.API.SCHEME:`NECESSARY.IP`}} or/and {{$NOMAD.CLIENT.API.PORT:`NECESSARY.IP`}} on master host or template level.
* Some metrics may not be collected depending on your HashiCorp Nomad agent version and configuration.

**Useful links**:

* [HashiCorp Nomad metrics list](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/docs/operations/metrics-reference)
* [HashiCorp Nomad telemetry configuration reference](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/docs/configuration/telemetry)
* [HashiCorp Nomad metrics API reference](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/api-docs/metrics)
* [HashiCorp Nomad nodes API reference](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/api-docs/nodes)
* [HashiCorp Nomad allocations API reference](https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/api-docs/allocations)
* [Zabbix user macros with context](https://www.zabbix.com/documentation/8.0/manual/config/macros/user_macros_context)

triggers.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Service [rpc] is down
triggers.description:
Cannot establish the connection to [rpc] service port {$NOMAD.CLIENT.RPC.PORT}.
Check the Nomad state and network connectivity between Nomad and Zabbix.

triggers.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Service [serf] is down
triggers.description:
Cannot establish the connection to [serf] service port {$NOMAD.CLIENT.SERF.PORT}.
Check the Nomad state and network connectivity between Nomad and Zabbix.

items.name: CPU allocated
items.description: Total amount of CPU shares the scheduler has allocated to tasks.

items.name: Disk allocated
items.description: Total amount of disk space the scheduler has allocated to tasks.

items.name: Memory allocated
items.description: Total amount of memory the scheduler has allocated to tasks.

items.name: Allocations blocked
items.description: Number of allocations waiting for previous versions.

items.name: Allocations migrating
items.description: Number of allocations migrating data from previous versions.

triggers.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: OOM killed allocations found

items.name: Allocations pending
items.description: Number of allocations pending (received by the client but not yet running).

triggers.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: High CPU utilization
triggers.event_name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: High CPU utilization: (over >= {$NOMAD.CPU.UTIL.MIN}% over last 10m)
triggers.description: CPU utilization is too high. The system might be slow to respond.

triggers.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Monitoring API connection has failed
triggers.event_name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Monitoring API connection has failed. HTTP response code: {{ITEM.VALUE}.regsub("HTTP\/[\d.]+\s+(\d{3})", \1")}. Response message: {{ITEM.VALUE}.regsub("HTTP\/[\d.]+\s+\d{3}\s+(.*)", \1")}
triggers.description:
Monitoring API connection has failed.
Ensure that Nomad API URL and the necessary permissions have been defined correctly, check the service state and network connectivity between Nomad and Zabbix.

triggers.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Allocations API connection has failed
triggers.event_name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Allocations API connection has failed. HTTP response code: {{ITEM.VALUE}.regsub("HTTP\/[\d.]+\s+(\d{3})", \1")}. Response message: {{ITEM.VALUE}.regsub("HTTP\/[\d.]+\s+\d{3}\s+(.*)", \1")}
triggers.description:
Allocations API connection has failed.
Ensure that Nomad API URL and the necessary permissions have been defined correctly, check the service state and network connectivity between Nomad and Zabbix.

dependencies.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Monitoring API connection has failed

items.name: Allocated jobs get
items.description: Allocated jobs data in raw format.

items.name: Memory available
items.description: Total amount of memory available to processes which includes free and cached memory.

items.name: Memory free
items.description: Amount of memory which is free.

items.name: Memory size
items.description: Total amount of physical memory on the node.

items.name: Memory used
items.description: Amount of memory used by processes.

items.name: Metrics
items.description: Nomad client metrics in raw format.

triggers.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Nodes API connection has failed
triggers.event_name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Nodes API connection has failed. HTTP response code: {{ITEM.VALUE}.regsub("HTTP\/[\d.]+\s+(\d{3})", \1")}. Response message: {{ITEM.VALUE}.regsub("HTTP\/[\d.]+\s+\d{3}\s+(.*)", \1")}
triggers.description:
Nodes API connection has failed.
Ensure that Nomad API URL and the necessary permissions have been defined correctly, check the service state and network connectivity between Nomad and Zabbix.

dependencies.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Monitoring API connection has failed

items.name: Node info get
items.description: Node info data in raw format.

items.name: CPU unallocated
items.description: Total amount of CPU shares free for the scheduler to allocate to tasks.

items.name: Disk unallocated
items.description: Total amount of disk space free for the scheduler to allocate to tasks.

items.name: Memory unallocated
items.description: Total amount of memory free for the scheduler to allocate to tasks.

items.name: Uptime
items.description: Uptime of the host running the Nomad client.

triggers.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: The host has been restarted
triggers.event_name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: The host has been restarted: (uptime < 10m)
triggers.description: The host uptime is less than 10 minutes.

triggers.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Nomad client version has changed
triggers.event_name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Nomad client version has changed to {ITEM.LASTVALUE}

item_prototypes.name: Job ["{#JOB.NAME}"] CPU allocated
item_prototypes.description: Total CPU resources allocated by the ["{#JOB.NAME}"] job across all cores.

item_prototypes.name: Job ["{#JOB.NAME}"] CPU system utilization
item_prototypes.description: Total CPU resources consumed by the ["{#JOB.NAME}"] job in system space.

item_prototypes.name: Job ["{#JOB.NAME}"] CPU throttled periods time
item_prototypes.description: Total number of CPU periods that the ["{#JOB.NAME}"] job was throttled.

item_prototypes.name: Job ["{#JOB.NAME}"] CPU throttled time
item_prototypes.description: Total time that the ["{#JOB.NAME}"] job was throttled.

item_prototypes.name: Job ["{#JOB.NAME}"] CPU total utilization
item_prototypes.description: Total CPU resources consumed by the ["{#JOB.NAME}"] job across all cores.

item_prototypes.name: Job ["{#JOB.NAME}"] CPU ticks
item_prototypes.description: CPU ticks consumed by the process for the ["{#JOB.NAME}"] job in the last collection interval.

item_prototypes.name: Job ["{#JOB.NAME}"] CPU user utilization
item_prototypes.description: Total CPU resources consumed by the ["{#JOB.NAME}"] job in user space.

item_prototypes.name: Job ["{#JOB.NAME}"] Memory allocated
item_prototypes.description: Amount of memory allocated by the ["{#JOB.NAME}"] job.

item_prototypes.name: Job ["{#JOB.NAME}"] Memory cached
item_prototypes.description: Amount of memory cached by the ["{#JOB.NAME}"] job.

item_prototypes.name: Job ["{#JOB.NAME}"] Memory swapped
item_prototypes.description: Amount of memory swapped by the ["{#JOB.NAME}"] job.

item_prototypes.name: Job ["{#JOB.NAME}"] Memory used
item_prototypes.description: Total amount of memory used by the ["{#JOB.NAME}"] job.

graph_prototypes.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Job ["{#JOB.NAME}"] task ["{#JOB.TASK.GROUP}"] namespace ["{#JOB.NAMESPACE}"] memory utilization
HashiCorp Nomad Client: Job [{#JOB.NAME}] task ["{#JOB.TASK.GROUP}"] namespace ["{#JOB.NAMESPACE}"] CPU throttling
HashiCorp Nomad Client: Job [{#JOB.NAME}] task ["{#JOB.TASK.GROUP}"] namespace ["{#JOB.NAMESPACE}"] CPU utilization

item_prototypes.name: Disk ["{#DEV.NAME}"] space available
item_prototypes.description: Amount of space which is available on ["{#DEV.NAME}"] disk.

item_prototypes.name: Disk ["{#DEV.NAME}"] inodes utilization
item_prototypes.description: Disk space consumed by the inodes on ["{#DEV.NAME}"] disk.

trigger_prototypes.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Running out of free inodes on [{#DEV.NAME}] device
trigger_prototypes.event_name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Running out of free inodes on [{#DEV.NAME}]  (free < {$NOMAD.INODES.FREE.MIN.CRIT:"{#DEV.NAME}"}%)
trigger_prototypes.description:
It may become impossible to write to a disk if there are no index nodes left.
The following error messages may be returned as symptoms, even though the free space:
- No space left on device;
- Disk is full.

trigger_prototypes.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Running out of free inodes on [{#DEV.NAME}] device
trigger_prototypes.event_name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Running out of free inodes on [{#DEV.NAME}]  (free < {$NOMAD.INODES.FREE.MIN.WARN:"{#DEV.NAME}"}%)
trigger_prototypes.description:
It may become impossible to write to a disk if there are no index nodes left.
The following error messages may be returned as symptoms, even though the free space:
- No space left on device;
- Disk is full.

dependencies.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Running out of free inodes on [{#DEV.NAME}] device

item_prototypes.name: Disk ["{#DEV.NAME}"] space used
item_prototypes.description: Amount of disk ["{#DEV.NAME}"] space which has been used.

trigger_prototypes.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: High disk [{#DEV.NAME}] utilization
trigger_prototypes.event_name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Disk [{#DEV.NAME}] space low (utilization: >= {$NOMAD.DISK.UTIL.MIN.CRIT:"{#DEV.NAME}"}%)

trigger_prototypes.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: High disk [{#DEV.NAME}] utilization
trigger_prototypes.event_name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Disk [{#DEV.NAME}] space low (utilization: >= {$NOMAD.DISK.UTIL.MIN.WARN:"{#DEV.NAME}"}%)

dependencies.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Running out of free inodes on [{#DEV.NAME}] device

trigger_prototypes.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Driver [{#DRIVER.NAME}] detection state has changed
trigger_prototypes.description: The [{#DRIVER.NAME}] driver detection state has changed.

trigger_prototypes.expression: last(/HashiCorp Nomad Client by HTTP/nomad.client.driver.state["{#DRIVER.NAME}"]) = 0 and last(/HashiCorp Nomad Client by HTTP/nomad.client.driver.detected["{#DRIVER.NAME}"]) = 1
trigger_prototypes.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: Driver [{#DRIVER.NAME}] is in unhealthy state
trigger_prototypes.description: The [{#DRIVER.NAME}] driver detected, but its state is unhealthy.

triggers.expression: (min(/HashiCorp Nomad Client by HTTP/nomad.client.memory.available, 10m) / last(/HashiCorp Nomad Client by HTTP/nomad.client.memory.total))*100 <= {$NOMAD.RAM.AVAIL.MIN}
triggers.name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: High memory utilization
triggers.event_name: HashiCorp Nomad Client: High memory utilization: (available < {$NOMAD.RAM.AVAIL.MIN}% over last 10m)
triggers.description: RAM utilization is too high. The system might be slow to respond.
